ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-18-2025 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-13-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Alison Merino, Esq., for Paul H. Nathan Jr., Petitioner.

The court at the outset recognizes the recently filed declarations that help rebut the 6124 presumptions. The court confirms that there are no appearances to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The lost will dated May-18-2001 is admitted to probate. Paul H. Nathan Jr. is appointed Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Karen Ann Simons is appointed probate Referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Mar-18-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Prado. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-23-2024 Continued To Feb-13-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Alison Merino appeared via zoom for Paul Nathan Jr. (petitioner).
David Brillant appeared via zoom for Juan Rosales (creditor).

Discussion was held regarding the original pour-over will, that the will was in the descendant's possession, and that Ms. Merino will prepare a declaration for the lost will and submit to the Courts. Petition for probate of will for letters testamentary is continued to February 13, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Papers to be filed by February 06. 2024.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Chavez. Not reported.
